string.text: Mexaminum radiation protection drugs are common in the Zone. When used, this drug induces contraction of peripheral blood vessels and oxygen deprivation, which serves to treat and prevent radiation exposure. The drug does not have severe side effects, although isolated cases of mild nausea, dizziness, cramps and stomach pain have been reported.

A normal elastic bandage used to stop bleeding and prevent infection of wounds. For regular bleeding, carefully apply the bandage to the wound. For arterial bleeding, urgently tie the bandage around the affected area.

A dense, sterile army-issue bandage. Designed for quick wound dressing and stopping heavy bleeding. Thanks to high-quality materials, it’s more effective than regular stalker bandages at dealing with injuries.

Standard issue military binoculars with a maximum of x7.5 magnification. Useful in the Zone in that they allow its owner to observe things from a distance %c[0,153,255,255](upgrade available)%c[default].
No bakers have ever been identified in the Zone, but this bread is neither contaminated nor radioactive, being fresh and quite edible. At least no complaints have been reported thus far.

Presumably, these canned goods were looted by stalkers from an army ration depot. Without any kind of label on the can, it's hard to say for sure. Should still be good to eat, if you believe the best before date.

This dated anomalous activity detector produces an alarm signal when anomalous particles enter a special chamber as you approach an anomaly. It can also register the presence of artefacts and measure the distance to the nearest one. The distance is indicated by the frequency of the sound the detector emits. To switch to artefact search mode, open the device's front cover. Due to its simple design, Echo can only be used to locate the most common artefacts.\n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Artefact detection rank: 1

A next generation scientific detection scanner. Thanks to three modernized detection chambers, the position of artefacts is shown on a special display screen. When closed, the device registers only radiation and anomalies. To switch to artefact search mode, open the front LED display. When in search mode, the detector is capable of locating all artefacts known to science.\n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Artefact detection ranks: 1-3

A prototype of an expanded functionality anomalous frequency scanner. This device comes with a program used to identify static areas of anomalous activity stored in its database, while retaining the ability to locate artefacts. The position of anomalous areas and artefacts is displayed on a special screen. The artefact search mode, which can be activated by opening the front LED display, has been modified to detect anomalous formations currently unknown to science.\n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Artefact detection ranks: 1-4
A standard head-mounted flashlight. Useful for stalkers who find themselves out and about after dark, but may attract trouble if used unwisely.

The IP2 antitoxin is a complex drug comprising several antidotes, including metoclopramidum, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboxin and other pharmaceuticals. The drug is used to inject a range of antibiotics to neutralize natural poisons, together with synthetic poison absorbers, into the bloodstream. IP2 is not particularly popular in the Zone, as most stalkers rely on a gas mask and a protective suit, there are still those who have a pack of this antidote stashed away. The effects of the drug are long-lasting.
Also known as sedatives or sleeping pills, is a class of medications that induces sleep. It is mainly used for treatment of insomnia and also in the administration of anesthesia.

A field medical stimulant, which is a complex of therapeutic agents such as: Trimadol (CAS: 27203-92-5) and Etamsylate (CAS: 2624-44-4). The main action is ultra-fast blood clotting and tissue regeneration.

A drug that blocks avalanche-like nerve impulses. Consumption induces short-term loss of all emotion. Used exclusively in the Zone to counteract the effects of anomalous psi-fields. The effects of the drug are long-lasting.

Often used by stalkers on long trips when no breaks can be afforded. The drink contains easily digestible carbohydrates, as well as drugs that stimulate the release of adrenaline into the bloodstream.
For better or worse, this sausage - a mix of chicken and a soy substitute - is often a stalker's breakfast, lunch and dinner in one. Can be stored for a long time due to high preservative content.

A specialized medical kit for providing first aid to combat casualties. The kit includes blood coagulants based on menadione, painkillers, antibiotics and immunostimulants, as well as a kit for removing bullets and fragments from the body. This medkit is issued to army personnel as standard and is sometimes used by Duty forces.
A general purpose single-use medical kit. Handy for treating various injuries - wounds, bruises, regular and chemical burns and various types of poisoning. In demand among stalkers.

A medical kit designed specifically for use in the Zone. The kit contains products for handling wounds, as well as preventing the development of radiation poisoning and reducing accumulated radiation in the body. Contains Vikasolum, Batilolum and Naphazoline, unlike other medkits.
Might have useful information on it.
Made by GSC. Goes down easy and considerably lowers the effects of radiation. Should be enjoyed in moderation.

This last generation anomalous activity detector can be used to locate anomalous formations and has a colour diode panel, which not only indicates the direction of artefacts, but also measures the distance to them. To switch to artefact search mode, open the device's front cover. Unfortunately, the device is programmed with a somewhat outdated list of detectable artefacts.\n \n%c[0,1,255,255]•%c[default] Artefact detection ranks: 1-2

An experimental drug developed by Professor Ozersky for scientific research teams working in the Zone. At its core, the drug contains tetrodotoxin, known colloquially as "zombie powder," which causes a complete shutdown of the body's central nervous system that may allow people to survive emissions outside cover. The drug has not undergone clinical or field testing, which puts its effectiveness in doubt and raises the issue of possible side effects.

The main component of this product is an anabolic androgen, an artificial steroid, the chemical composition of which resembles testosterone. Often used by stalkers during long raids to reduce muscle fatigue. Significantly increases weight-carrying capacity. The effects of the drug are long-lasting.

B190 Indraline radioprotectant, included in medkits issued to nuclear power plant personnel. Used in the Zone instead of the excessively toxic PC1 cystamine hydrochloride, repeated use of which often caused death. Reduces the effects of gamma radiation on live tissue. Best consumed before exposure, as effects are not instant. The effects of the drug are long-lasting.
For blues and prison romance lovers.

This belt-fed machine gun is a portable modification with a complex two-stage feeding system. Despite its weight and low precision, this LMG has found admirers in the Zone. Used primarily to fight off outnumbering enemy forces during faction conflicts and clear out mutant lairs.
C-4 charge with a block detonator
Explosive charge. Comes with a remote control. C4 explosive is stable and can only be detonated by a combination of extreme temperatures and shock waves from the detonator.
Used for remote detonation of the C-4 charge after installation.
Regular mineral water, good for quenching thirst and used as a chaser for vodka.
One of the most popular bars in the world, tasty and nutritious.

Original army flask of the Russian or Soviet warehouse storage. All stalkers will appreciate the simple yet clever and lightweight construction of this flask. Moreover, the price of the flask is more than affordable.
The flask is a container traditionally used for strong alcoholic beverages such as brandy, rum, whiskey, vodka. However, it can also be used for other liquids. The flask tightly screws on the thread.
One of the world's most famous brands of energy drinks. Valued by stalkers for a stronger surge of energy, but at the same time, it "bites" with its price compared to the "stalker" energy drink.
A regular water resistant backpack. It has high popularity among stalkers who travel around the Zone %c[0,1,255,255](low loss of stamina).
Small backpack usually worn by rookies. The inside has been modified to keep water out. Not a pretty solution, but works %c[0,1,255,255](very low loss of stamina).
Backpack used by the military on longer assaults %c[0,1,255,255](average loss of stamina).
A tourist backpack with a lot of storage space. Not very popular due to it's size %c[0,1,255,255](above-average loss of stamina).
It's a dead piece of hardware. No reception, no transmission, just junk. Can only be used as a souvenir from the old days.
The handle's cracked, the blade's covered in rust. No cutting or stabbing - just for the junk collection.
One lens is cracked, the other lens has fallen out and the mechanism is stuck. It is possible to use it, but doing that is as useless as squinting into the fog.
The broken voice of the Zone. Torn fur, buttons sticking. No music - only memories from someone's last campfire.
The screen is cracked and the case is melted. It won't turn on. Maybe It had seen too much, and the Zone decided that was enough.
It has a damaged sensor and has lost its calibration. Detects anomalies with a long delay or ignores them altogether. Cannot be used.
An encrypted PDA to take to Nimble on a mission from Spore (Clear Sky trader).
